title: lasthenia minor subsp. maritima
intro: Lasthenia minor subsp. maritima, also known as sea everlasting or coastal goldfields, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lasthenia minor subsp. maritima include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. In herbal preparations, it can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and is often used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wound care.
